summary: Large closed, unmerged WIP PR that moved the fighter text section from asm/melee/ft/fighter.s into src/melee/ft/fighter.c while preserving buildability through inline assembly and optional C alternatives. The author framed it as a coordination/synchronization step: keep fighter.h in sync, expose all fighter functions in C form so contributors could decompile individual functions out of order, and avoid linking fighter.c until all functions matched. The PR also expanded fighter.h, touched global function/variable headers, added math fpclassify support, and adjusted nearby fighter/player call sites. Review feedback focused on avoiding unnecessary sysdolphin struct field renaming and tightening function prototype types.
what_changed: Deleted almost the entire text section from asm/melee/ft/fighter.s, leaving the assembly file primarily as an include/data holder rather than the location of fighter functions.
Added a new src/melee/ft/fighter.c with roughly 7830 added lines, containing all fighter.s functions, mostly as inline assembly.
Kept data in fighter.s while moving functions into fighter.c, according to the PR body: "All data is still in fighter.s, but all functions are in fighter.c."
Expanded src/melee/ft/fighter.h substantially, including a much larger Fighter struct definition area and a list of function prototypes for functions from fighter.s.

Changed include/functions.h and include/variables.h to move or synchronize many fighter-related declarations, though the author noted that some external prototypes and variables were still parked in fighter.c because their proper types were not known.
Added fpclassify-related math support to include/math.h; the author said this should eventually live in math.h and was the same as in pikmin2.
Adjusted small call-site or prototype-related uses in character/fighter files such as ftclink.c, ftdrmario.c, ftdrmario_2.c, ftcliffcommon.c, and ftwalljump.c.
Touched player declarations/definitions in src/melee/pl/player.h and src/melee/pl/player.c, likely to align prototypes used by fighter.c.
Added fighter.c to build-related lists in e_files.mk and obj_files.mk, while the author explicitly warned fighter.c should not be linked until all functions are done.
smart_moves: Used inline asm as a staging bridge: this preserved the ability to build while moving every fighter function into a C translation unit where individual functions could be replaced with matching C later.

Structured fighter.c into searchable sections marked with separators; the author explicitly told reviewers contributors could CTRL-F for "=======" to find fpclassify, external functions, external variables, fighter.c variables, and fighter.c functions.
Allowed C alternatives to be ifdef'ed in and out, enabling out-of-order decompilation without forcing all of fighter.c to match at once.

Kept unknown external prototypes and variables local to fighter.c temporarily rather than prematurely polluting global headers with bad types; the author asked whether these should eventually move to functions.h and variables.h.
Separated function migration from data migration: keeping data in fighter.s reduced risk while moving text to fighter.c.
Used the PR to synchronize fighter.h with the repository so future decomp contributors had a more complete struct/prototype base.
decomp_lessons: For very large modules, an inline-asm C staging file can unblock parallel decompilation: move all functions into a C file first, then replace individual functions with C under ifdefs as they match.
Do not link a staged C file too early if data loading or remaining matching issues are unresolved; the author explicitly stated fighter.c should not be linked until all functions are done.

Keep header scope disciplined during staging. fighter.h should contain only declarations that belong to fighter, while uncertain external prototypes should either remain local temporarily or be moved to functions.h once their types are known.

Large struct headers can be useful even with provisional names, but unverified external naming should be marked as such; the author noted many Fighter struct variables still used unverified Akaneia names.

Avoid renaming fields in unrelated core/sysdolphin structs just to encode offsets; reviewer feedback said sysdolphin-related things did not need this and comments about position would be handled separately.
Prefer precise pointer types in function prototypes once known. Review suggested changing func_800693AC's first argument from void* to HSD_GObj*.
When migrating from asm to C, preserve searchability and contributor workflow with clear section headers and explicit TODO lists.
naming_conventions: Fighter struct fields in the excerpt used offset-prefixed provisional names such as x2358_stateVar7, reflecting the common decomp convention of encoding offsets when semantic names are unknown.
The author described many Fighter struct variables as still having unverified Akaneia names, implying those names were provisional rather than authoritative.

Reviewer PsiLupan pushed back on renaming sysdolphin HSD_JObj field flags to x14_flags, indicating that sysdolphin structs should not necessarily use offset-prefixed field names when established names exist.
Function symbols remained address-style placeholders such as func_800679B0, func_80067A84, func_80067ABC, func_80067BB4, func_80067C98, and func_800693AC.
Global labels and variables remained address-style labels such as lbl_80458FD0, lbl_803C10D0, and lbl_804D64F8 where semantics were not yet established.
Reviewer suggestion used HSD_GObj* for a fighter function object parameter, reinforcing use of concrete engine object types instead of void* when the argument type is known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted a large assembly text section into inline assembly inside fighter.c rather than attempting a full C match in one PR.
Kept all functions present in fighter.c, with C alternatives switchable by ifdefs so partial matching work could proceed independently.
Retained fighter data sections in fighter.s to reduce relocation/data-order risk while text functions were migrated.
Added or staged declarations for extern functions and variables near the C file until types could be confirmed.
Used build-file changes to include the new C object in project metadata while documenting that final linking should wait until all functions were done.

The diff excerpt shows direct migration of functions beginning at func_800679B0, including calls to HSD_ObjAllocInit, func_800852B0, func_8008549C, func_8009F4A4, func_800C8064, and func_800C8F6C, preserving original control flow while enabling future C replacement.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Do not rename sysdolphin-related struct fields just to add offset information; comments about position can be added separately. This was in response to changing HSD_JObj field flags to x14_flags.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested a more specific prototype for func_800693AC: void func_800693AC(HSD_GObj*, s32, s32, s32, f32, f32, f32); instead of using void* for the first argument.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that actually merged fighter.c or replaced this WIP inline-asm staging approach.
Search for func_800693AC to confirm whether the HSD_GObj* prototype was adopted.
Search for HSD_JObj flags/x14_flags to confirm sysdolphin field naming convention after review.
Search for fpclassify in include/math.h and other projects such as pikmin2 to verify final shared implementation.
Search later fighter.h changes for removal or replacement of unverified Akaneia Fighter field names.
Search for PRs that migrate fighter data out of fighter.s after this function-only migration attempt.
